'''Adam Milner''' (born 19 December 1991) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} or {{rlp|hk}} for [[Oldham RLFC|Oldham]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. He has played for [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He had previously played the majority of his professional club career with the [[Castleford Tigers]]. Across 14 seasons, he made over 300 appearances, with only [[Michael Shenton]] and [[Nathan Massey (rugby league, born 1989)|Nathan Massey]] having registered more for the Tigers in the Super League era. He spent two seasons at the [[Huddersfield Giants]] before moving to the Championship with Oldham.

== Background ==
Milner was born in [[Wakefield]], West Yorkshire, England.

Milner played junior rugby league for [[Stanley Rangers]].

== Club career ==

=== Castleford Tigers ===

==== Breakthrough ====
Having come through the Tigers' academy system, Milner made his [[Super League]] début against [[Huddersfield Giants]] on Sunday 4 July 2010.<ref>{{cite news|title=Castleford Tigers 44-18 Huddersfield Giants|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/8776138.stm|work= [[BBC Sport]]|date=4 July 2010|access-date=14 September 2010}}</ref>

Milner scored his first try for Castleford against [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] on 21 August 2010.<ref>{{cite news|title=Castleford 40-28 Harlequins|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/8921988.stm|work= [[BBC Sport]]|date=22 August 2010|access-date=14 September 2010}}</ref> He followed up his début try with another one in the following league game against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], in the last match at [[Knowsley Road]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/8963473.stm|title=St Helens 40-30 Castleford|date=2010-09-04|work=[[BBC Sport]]|access-date=2020-04-25|language=en-GB}}</ref> He made 4 appearances and scored 2 tries in his first season.

Prior to the 2011 season, Milner was given the number 16 shirt, with Castleford head coach [[Terry Matterson]] saying "I think he's going to get plenty of first team footy this year."<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.superleaguefans.com/castleford-tigers/2011/01/19/2011-castleford-tigers-squad-numbers.html|title=2011 Castleford Tigers squad numbers|date=2011-01-19|website=Superleaguefans.com|url-status=live|access-date=2020-04-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110524065523/http://www.superleaguefans.com/castleford-tigers/2011/01/19/2011-castleford-tigers-squad-numbers.html |archive-date=24 May 2011 }}</ref> He made 22 appearances and scored 4 tries.<ref name="RLRKC" /> At the end of the season, he signed a new three-year deal with the Tigers.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/15197850|title=New deal for Castleford Tigers hooker Adam Milner|date=2011-10-06|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-26|language=en-gb}}</ref>

==== First team hooker ====
Having firmly established himself in Castleford's first team, Milner made 21 appearances and scored 3 tries in the [[2012 Castleford Tigers season|2012 season]].

2013 was Milner's highest scoring season yet for Castleford, with 7 tries in 23 appearances.<ref name="RLRKC" /> In August, he signed a two-year contract extension until the end of 2016, saying, "As soon as I got the contract offer I didn't think twice about signing it."<ref name="2013 Contract">{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23880029|title=Adam Milner extends Castleford Tigers contract|date=2013-08-29|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-26|language=en-gb}}</ref> Head coach [[Daryl Powell]] said, "both his attacking game and his toughness in defence are developing every week and I know he will continue to work hard to achieve his undoubted potential."<ref name="2013 Contract" />

Milner was awarded for his consistent performances at hooker with squad number 9 for the 2014 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-2014-squad-numbers/|title=Castleford Tigers 2014 squad numbers|date=2013-12-19|website=Love Rugby League|language=en-GB|access-date=2020-04-26}}</ref> He made a further 25 appearances and scored 3 tries to help Castleford to a 4th placed finish in Super League.<ref name="RLRKC" />

He played in all but 3 of the Tigers' matches in 2015, playing more minutes and taking a larger role in the team following the departure of [[Man of Steel Awards|Man of Steel]]-winning hooker [[Daryl Clark]] to [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the end of the previous season. He made 28 appearances and scored 3 tries.<ref name="RLRKC" />

In February 2016, Milner signed a two-year contract extension, keeping him with the Tigers until the end of 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/milner-signs-extension/|title=Milner signs extension|date=2016-02-09|website=Love Rugby League|language=en-GB|access-date=2020-04-25}}</ref> He was ever-present throughout Castleford's injury-struck [[2016 Castleford Tigers season|2016 season]], appearing in all 32 matches.<ref name="RLRKC" /> He mostly played as a hooker, but switched position to loose forward towards the end of the campaign due to mounting injuries in the squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/adam-milner/summary.html|title=Adam Milner Career Stats & Summary|website=Rugby League Project|url-status=live|access-date=2020-04-25|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121109151525/http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/adam-milner/summary.html |archive-date=9 November 2012 }}</ref> At the club's end-of-season awards night, Milner was named Directors' Player of the Year and 2nd place Player of the Year.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/moors-scoops-hat-trick-player-year-awards-2131405|title=Moors scoops hat-trick of player of the year awards|date=2016-09-21|work=The Pontefract & Castleford Express|access-date=2020-04-25}}</ref>

==== Evolving role ====
For the 2017 campaign, Milner was assigned squad number 13, indicating his switch in position to loose forward.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-squad-numbers-revealed-748853|title=Castleford Tigers squad numbers revealed|date=2017-01-14|work=The Pontefract & Castleford Express|access-date=2020-04-26}}</ref> He played a crucial role in Castleford's play-off semi-final golden point victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] - just minutes after making a try-saving tackle on [[Tommy Makinson]], he burrowed his way over the line for a try under the sticks.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/11058369/castleford-23-22-st-helens-aet-luke-gale-the-hero-with-golden-drop-goal|title=Castleford 23-22 St Helens (AET): Luke Gale the hero with golden drop-goal|website=Sky Sports|language=en|access-date=2020-04-25}}</ref> He was voted as the Fans' Man of the Match for his efforts. He also played in the [[2017 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2017/oct/07/castleford-v-leeds-grand-final-2017-live-super-league-rugby?page=with:block-59d9128de4b00593b4d67a05#liveblog-navigation|title=Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened|work=The Guardian|date=7 October 2017|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/41536394|title=Grand Final 2017: Castleford 6-24 Leeds Rhinos|date=7 October 2017|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/oct/07/castleford-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Danny McGuire guides Leeds to Grand Final success over Castleford|date=7 October 2017|work=The Guardian|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref>

Milner kicked the first goal of his career on Castleford's visit to the [[Warrington Wolves]] on 8 June 2018, in the absence of the Tigers' first choice 5 kickers.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44368358|title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 30-34 Castleford Tigers|date=2018-06-08|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-25|language=en-gb}}</ref> In July 2018, Milner signed a new three-year contract with the Tigers, keeping him at the club until the end of 2021. This was despite a number of [[National Rugby League|NRL]] clubs declaring an interest in him as a result of his impressive form.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44898034|title=Adam Milner: Castleford hooker spurns NRL interest to sign new three-year deal|date=2018-07-20|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-25|language=en-gb}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/big-career-choice-lies-ahead-castleford-tigers-demand-adam-milner-704648|title=Big career choice lies ahead for Castleford Tigers' in-demand Adam Milner|date=2018-06-05|work=The Yorkshire Post|access-date=2020-04-26}}</ref> Castleford director of rugby Jon Wells said: "I think it sends a clear and bold statement about our ambitions as a club and I am delighted that Adam will now move into the best years of his career as a Castleford Tiger."

It was Milner's try from dummy half against the [[Wigan Warriors]] on 5 April 2019 that completed the Tigers' comeback from a 20-point deficit, in a game they went on to win.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/11685332/castleford-38-28-wigan-zak-hardaker-endures-miserable-return-to-the-jungle|title=Castleford 38-28 Wigan: Zak Hardaker endures miserable return to the Jungle|date=2019-04-06|website=Sky Sports|language=en|access-date=2020-04-26}}</ref> He opened the scoring in Castleford's Elimination final victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the play-offs, touching down a deft [[Paul McShane (rugby league)|Paul McShane]] kick.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11814190/warrington-wolves-12-14-castleford-tigers-visitors-hold-on-for-super-league-play-off-win|title=Warrington Wolves 12-14 Castleford Tigers: Visitors hold on for Super League play-off win|date=2019-09-20|website=Sky Sports|language=en|access-date=2020-04-26}}</ref> In this year, he made 28 appearances and scored 3 tries.

Going into the 2020 season, Milner said he felt "fitter, faster and stronger" and talked of his desire to further improve his performances.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-adam-milner-feeling-fitter-faster-and-stronger-1743333|title=Castleford Tigers' Adam Milner feeling fitter, faster and stronger|date=2020-01-30|work=The Yorkshire Post|access-date=2020-04-26}}</ref> He scored against the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in Castleford's first match of the campaign.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11924635/toronto-wolfpack-10-28-castleford-tigers-tigers-crash-wolfpacks-super-league-debut-party|title=Toronto Wolfpack 10-28 Castleford Tigers: Tigers crash Wolfpack's Super League debut party|date=2020-02-03|website=Sky Sports|language=en|url-status=live|access-date=2020-04-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200204232258/https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11924635/toronto-wolfpack-10-28-castleford-tigers-tigers-crash-wolfpacks-super-league-debut-party |archive-date=4 February 2020 }}</ref>

==== Veteran ====
Milner was granted a testimonial year in 2021, and took on [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] at [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]] for his testimonial match on 14 March.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-02-14|title=Milner testimonial game announced|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7261|access-date=2022-02-20|website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> On 17 July 2021, he played for Castleford in their [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57828390|title=Challenge Cup: St Helens beat Castleford 26-12 for first Wembley victory since 2008|work=BBC Sport }}</ref> In August, it was announced that Milner had signed a new two-year deal with Castleford; "To remain a one-club man, which is something I pride myself on, is a proud moment, I am excited for what is to come," he said.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-08-16|title=Adam Milner: Castleford hooker agrees new deal with club|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58237029|access-date=2022-02-20|website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

The 2022 season saw Milner assume squad number 12 due to the return of [[Joe Westerman]] to Castleford.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2022-01-21|title=Tigers reveal 2022 squad numbers|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7841|access-date=2022-02-20|website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> He scored a try against [[Toulouse Olympique]] on 1 April after recovering from a back injury sustained in pre-season which delayed his start to the campaign.<ref>{{Cite web |date=1 April 2022 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 32-6 Toulouse - Hosts register second win in two matches |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/60926889 |access-date=23 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref name="Radford">{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=2022-04-01 |title=Castleford Tigers: Adam Milner enjoying fresh change under Lee Radford |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-adam-milner-enjoying-lee-radford-coaching/ |access-date=2022-04-03 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> On playing under new head coach [[Lee Radford]], Milner said, "I think bringing a new coach in has certainly helped the environment and the team go in a bit of a different direction."<ref name="Radford" /> Milner finished the season with 19 appearances, having featured at both {{Rlp|sr}} and {{Rlp|hk}}.

In 2023, Milner returned to squad number 16 after last wearing it in 2011.<ref>{{Cite web |date=20 January 2023 |title=Tigers announce squad numbers for 2023 |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8438 |access-date=23 August 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> His performance against the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in Castleford's first victory of the 2023 season saw him named in the Super League Team of the Week.<ref>{{Cite web |date=20 March 2023 |title=Round 5 Team of the Week |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/3355/round-5-team-of-the-week |access-date=23 August 2023 |website=Super League}}</ref> On 24 March, he made his 300th career appearance.<ref>{{Cite web |date=24 March 2023 |title=Defeat for the Tigers in round 6 |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8529 |access-date=23 August 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> In April, [[Wakefield Trinity]] enquired about Milner's availability but Castleford declined the move.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=12 April 2023 |title=Castleford boss provides update on Adam Milner future |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-boss-provides-update-on-adam-milner-future |access-date=23 August 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> On 5 May, he made his 300th Castleford appearance.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=25 May 2023 |title=Adam Milner set to leave Castleford Tigers to join Huddersfield Giants |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/adam-milner-set-to-leave-castleford-tigers-to-join-huddersfield-giants-4157878 |access-date=23 August 2023 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref>

On 25 May 2023, Milner confirmed that he would leave the Castleford Tigers with immediate effect. He said, "I am immensely proud to have worn the Cas colours and represent this amazing club for so long. I'm gutted the way it has ended and it has been an incredibly emotional time for myself to leave, but I am looking forward to a new challenge at Huddersfield."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=25 May 2023 |title=Adam Milner breaks silence on emotional Castleford Tigers exit as he confirms next Super League club |url=https://www.totalrl.com/adam-milner-breaks-silence-on-emotional-castleford-tigers-exit-as-he-confirms-next-super-league-club/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230610113606/https://www.totalrl.com/adam-milner-breaks-silence-on-emotional-castleford-tigers-exit-as-he-confirms-next-super-league-club/ |archive-date=10 June 2023 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Total RL}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Gordon |first=James |date=25 May 2023 |title=Long-serving Castleford forward set to make immediate Super League move |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/long-serving-castleford-forward-set-to-make-immediate-super-league-move |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

=== Huddersfield Giants ===
On 19 May 2023, [[Huddersfield Giants]] head coach [[Ian Watson (rugby league)|Ian Watson]] first confirmed his interest in signing Milner, who was in the final year of his [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] contract.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=19 May 2023 |title=Huddersfield boss confirms interest in Castleford Tigers utility |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-boss-confirms-interest-in-castleford-tigers-utility |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> The next week, on 25 May, the Giants announced the signing of Milner, initially on a season-long loan deal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=25 May 2023 |title=Adam Milner: Huddersfield Giants sign Castleford Tigers stalwart on initial loan |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65710385 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> This would be made permanent at the end of the season, on a one-year deal with an option for a further year.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=2023-05-25 |title=Adam Milner's Huddersfield switch confirmed, with interesting contract details |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/adam-milners-huddersfield-switch-confirmed-with-interesting-contract-details |access-date=2023-05-25 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He was ineligible for Huddersfield's game against Castleford the following day, and made his debut on 4 June against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at the [[Magic Weekend#2023: Newcastle|Magic Weekend]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Newsum |first=Matt |date=4 June 2023 |title=Super League: St Helens 48-6 Huddersfield Giants - Saints move into top six |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65754143 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In the build up towards his [[Wheldon Road]] return in August, Milner admitted, "It's not just another game," and said, "It'll be nice to see some familiar faces, not just the playing squad but the backroom staff, supporters and sponsors."<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=3 August 2023 |title='It's not just another game': Adam Milner juggling mixed emotions ahead of Castleford Tigers return with Huddersfield Giants |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/its-not-just-another-game-adam-milner-juggling-mixed-emotions-ahead-of-castleford-tigers-return-with-huddersfield-giants-4241560 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> He went on to score his first Huddersfield try against his former club, crossing late on to confirm a resounding victory over the Tigers.<ref>{{Cite web |date=4 August 2023 |title=Super League: Castleford 0-28 Huddersfield - Sam Halsall hat-trick as Giants win fourth in a row |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66397936 |access-date=29 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

=== Oldham RLFC ===
On 26 September 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=26 Sep 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Ex-Castleford Tigers and Huddersfield Giants veteran Adam Milner’s new club confirmed|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-castleford-tigers-and-huddersfield-giants-veteran-adam-milners-new-club-confirmed/}}</ref>

== International career ==
Milner represented England Schools U16s on their tour of France in Easter 2008. He played as a hooker in the 26-24 defeat by [[France national rugby league team|France U16s]] in [[Tonneins]], scoring the first try of the series and receiving a Man of the Match award. He played as a hooker in the 36-8 victory over [[France national rugby league team|France U16s]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.stanleyrangers.org.uk/index1.htm|title=Adam Milner - England Schools Under 16s tour|date=2008-04-05|website=Stanley Rangers|url-status=live|access-date=2020-04-25|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20030213002057/http://www.stanleyrangers.org.uk:80/index1.htm |archive-date=13 February 2003 }}</ref>

Milner captained the England Academy in 2010.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=516|title=England Academy captain ready for Australian Challenge|date=2010-12-03|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2020-04-26}}</ref> He represented England Academy as a hooker in the 40-16 victory over [[France national rugby league team|France U18s]] at the [[Stade Max Rousie|Stade Max Rousié]] on 9 June 2010, and scored a try.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rlef.eu.com/fixtures/326|title=France 16-40 England|website=www.rlef.eu.com|access-date=2020-04-26}}</ref> He played as a hooker in the 38-30 victory over [[Australian Institute of Sport]] (AIS) at the [[Leigh Sports Village]] on 4 December 2010, and the 34-22 victory over [[Australian Institute of Sport]] (AIS) at [[Leigh Sports Village]] on 11 December 2010.<ref name="Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=964|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|date=31 December 2013|accessdate=1 January 2014|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120512162028/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=964|archivedate=12 May 2012}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2010/12/06/england-academy-wins-first-test/|title=England Academy wins first Test|date=2010-12-05|website=National Rugby League|language=en|access-date=2020-04-26}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/england-academy-squad-named/|title=England academy squad named|date=2010-12-09|website=Love Rugby League|language=en-GB|access-date=2020-04-26}}</ref>

In July 2018, Milner was called up to the [[England national rugby league team|England]] Elite Performance Squad for the second phase of the season, in the build up towards the International Test Series against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] later in the year.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/sam-tomkins-called-up-to-england-squad-along-with-adam-milner-and-mike-cooper/|title=Sam Tomkins called up to England squad along with Adam Milner and Mike Cooper|last=Darbyshire|first=Drew|date=2018-07-24|website=Love Rugby League|language=en-GB|access-date=2020-04-25}}</ref> He made his senior international début on 17 October 2018 in [[England national rugby league team|England]]'s 44-6 victory against [[France national rugby league team|France]] at the [[Leigh Sports Village]].<ref name="Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2018/10/17/tom-johnstone-marks-debut-england-hat-trick-against-france/|title=Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France|work=The Guardian|date=17 October 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/45863248|title=England 44-6 France: Tom Johnstone scores hat-trick in warm-up for New Zealand Test series|date=2018-10-17|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-25|language=en-gb}}</ref> He represented [[England national rugby league team|England]] in their 20-14 victory over [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at [[Anfield]] on 4 November 2018, and in their 0-34 loss to [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at [[Elland Road]] on 11 November 2018 in their three-match test series.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/46090584|title=England 20-14 New Zealand: Tommy Makinson's hat-trick seals the series|date=2018-11-04|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2020-04-26|language=en-gb}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11551747/england-0-34-new-zealand-wayne-bennetts-side-thrashed|title=England 0-34 New Zealand: Wayne Bennett's side thrashed|date=2018-11-11|website=Sky Sports|language=en|url-status=live|access-date=2020-04-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191009024720/https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11551747/england-0-34-new-zealand-wayne-bennetts-side-thrashed |archive-date=9 October 2019 }}</ref>
